description_long: This object is a large solar energy infrastructure installation, featuring multiple arrays of photovoltaic panels mounted on a flat, rectangular platform. The panels are arranged in distinct clusters, with some sections tilted at an angle to optimize sunlight capture. The platform appears to be constructed from a combination of metal and concrete, with visible support structures beneath the solar panels. There are several raised and recessed areas on the platform, possibly for maintenance access or housing electrical components. The overall design suggests a rooftop or ground-mounted solar installation suitable for urban environments. The color palette is dominated by shades of gray, black, and blue, giving it a modern, industrial appearance.
description: A large, multi-array solar panel installation on a flat platform with angled panels and visible support structures, primarily in gray, black, and blue tones.
description_view_0: From this side view, the multiple tilted solar panel arrays and the flat, expansive platform are clearly visible, highlighting the structure's layered design.
description_view_1: This end view emphasizes the height and tilt of the solar panels, showing the staggered arrangement and the depth of the supporting platform.
description_view_2: The opposite side view reveals the underside of the panels and more of the platform's surface features, including recessed sections and additional support elements.
description_view_3: From this end, the clustered arrangement of the solar panels and the stepped design of the platform are most apparent, with clear visibility of the panel supports.
attribute_panel_orientation: Panels are tilted at approximately 25-35 degrees, with the majority facing a consistent direction; orientation appears fixed.
attribute_mounting_structure_type: roof-mounted or platform-mounted with visible metal (likely aluminum or steel) support frames.
attribute_array_configuration: Multiple clusters of panels arranged in two main groups, each with 4-8 panels, forming a staggered, non-linear configuration.
